source: pred.gg public profile + own aggregate baselines

archetype.label: The Wanderer
archetype.receipt: top three heroes are only 36% of 748 games — spreading games this thin slows the climb in every queue.

entries.change: Move the midlane games to carry
Queue carry instead of the usual mix
entries.receipt: 55.1% there vs 51.5% overall, 170 games of evidence
52.7% over 95 games on Serath vs 51.5% overall

ledger.note: wins per 100 games spent on each change; entries overlap and do not sum

plan: Queue carry as primary. It is Cuban's best role at 55.1% over 170 games (overall is 51.5%). Set midlane as secondary, not the other way around.
Stop queueing midlane: 46.0% over 200 games is costing real VP. When auto-filled there, play the safest comfort pick, not a learning pick.
Cuban's champion pool is 10+ heroes wide and the top three are only 36% of the games. Gold-to-Platinum climbs are almost always pool-narrowing stories: aim for 70%+ of games on the top three.
The math: Cuban is at 675 VP; Platinum III starts at 900. The all-time peak is 824.
honesty: personal winrates are shrunk toward the player's own average (small heater samples do not count as mains)
field baselines are all-ranks aggregates from our current-patch match sample, not Gold-bracket-specific yet
winrate edges are observational; they say where the player wins, not why

insights.title: Goldilocks324 brings out Cuban's best
insights.finding: Cuban's record alongside Goldilocks324 is 56% over 205 ranked games — the strongest pairing in the stack. When only two of the squad queue, make it this pair.

insights.title: For the group chat
insights.finding: 2 pentakills in real 5v5s and a longest spree of 21 (plus 18 more in ARAM and brawls — the group chat can rule on whether those count). Cuban has receipts.

insights.title: Cuban's lean, relative to the squad: objective damage
insights.finding: Cuban's profile sits mid-pack on most axes; the clearest lean is objective damage at 1.1x the squad average. Steady is a style too — the lineup can build around predictability.

coachReasoning.assessment: Cuban's two recent carry wins were the best games in the sample — Sparrow 10/3/5 at 1188 hero damage per minute against a 901 norm, and GRIM.exe 17/1/3 with one death against a 4-death norm — while the jungle and midlane games all lost. The biggest lever is playing more of exactly those carry games, with Sparrow (68.5% over 73 games) as the spearhead.
plan: Make Sparrow the default carry pick and drill the back-of-the-fight positioning from the 10/3/5 win — keep arrows flowing from behind the frontline all fight; 68.5% over 73 games says the pattern already works.
Bottle the GRIM.exe game: 17/1/3 with 1 death against a 4-death norm — taking only the shots that cost nothing is what the 60.0% carry record over 170 games looks like.

On Serath, fix the 3/5/2 jungle loss: damage came out at 450 per minute against a 675 norm, the mark of diving first and dying early — go in second, after the enemy has spent their stuns, and the 58.9% over 95 games version of Serath comes back.

Retire Countess for ranked at 37.9% over 87 games — when midlane comes up anyway, take Aurora (54.7% over 86 games) or Zinx, and after the 4/6/11 Zinx loss (6 deaths against a 3-death norm), drill trading from behind the minion wave instead of on top of it.
Midlane is the one queue clearly costing VP at 46.0% over 200 games — moving those games to carry is priced at +6.2 wins per 100, the largest priced change on the list.

insights.title: The carry/midlane split
insights.finding: Carry sits at 60.0% over 170 games while midlane sits at 46.0% over 200 — the same player, two very different results.

insights.title: Sparrow is the outlier
insights.finding: 68.5% over 73 games — the highest winrate in Cuban's pool, and the recent 10/3/5 win showed why.

insights.title: The duo call
insights.finding: Alongside Goldilocks324 the record is 56% over 205 ranked games — the strongest pairing in the stack.

insights.title: The Wanderer tax
insights.finding: The top three heroes are only 36% of 748 games — spreading games that thin slows the climb in every queue.

coachReasoningSource: in-session pred-scout-coach agent, grounded in the report + the player’s recent reviewed games; every number ground-checked, failing lines dropped
